FLR.title: Dow Tumbles Over 300 Points: Investor Sentiment Declines, Fear Index Remains In 'Greed' Zone

MCD.title: Dow Tumbles Over 300 Points: Investor Sentiment Declines, Fear Index Remains In 'Greed' Zone

macro_events.event_summary: Trump threatens "much higher" tariffs on the EU if a trade deal isn't signed by a new deadline, escalating transatlantic trade tensions.
primary_drivers: Tariff cost pass-through on EU imports raises COGS for US importers and reduces margins for EU exporters with US revenue exposure
Retaliation risk for US exporters into EU (agriculture, aircraft, spirits, tech)
Domestic substitution / reshoring tailwind for US producers competing with EU imports

beneficiaries.rationale: Stellantis has significant US manufacturing footprint vs. pure-EU peers; though European-domiciled, its Detroit-based Jeep/Ram production is largely tariff-insulated, gaining relative share if BMW/Mercedes/Audi imports get tariffed. Caveat: STLA is also EU-listed exposure, so confidence trimmed.

Ford's nearly fully domestic US production (F-150, Bronco, Explorer) directly competes with European luxury/premium imports (BMW, Mercedes, Audi, Porsche, VW). EU tariffs raise competitor pricing, supporting Ford ASPs and share in crossovers/SUVs.
Same domestic-production thesis as Ford; Cadillac/Buick benefit if European luxury imports face tariff-driven price hikes. Minimal EU revenue exposure means low retaliation risk.

Tesla's US-built Model Y/3/S/X gain pricing power vs. tariffed German EVs (Porsche Taycan, Mercedes EQ, BMW i-series, Audi e-tron). Berlin Gigafactory production for European market is unaffected by US import tariffs.

MGP Ingredients (American whiskey/bourbon distiller) gains domestic share if EU spirits (Scotch via Diageo, Irish whiskey, French cognac) face retaliatory escalation that disrupts the existing tariff truce on spirits. Pure-play US distiller.

losers.rationale: Diageo's Scotch whisky (Johnnie Walker) and gin portfolio is UK/EU-produced and US-imported; ~45% of operating profit from North America. Prior 2019-21 tariff cycle showed direct earnings hit. Retaliation risk if EU counters.

Boeing is the canonical EU retaliation target (Airbus subsidy dispute history). EU could impose retaliatory tariffs or accelerate Airbus orders at Boeing's expense. 2019 precedent saw direct measures.
Deere has meaningful EU agricultural and construction equipment exports; retaliatory tariffs on US ag machinery would directly hit a known target category from prior trade disputes.

Constellation Brands imports Italian and other European wines (Robert Mondavi imports, prior portfolio); though Mexican beer is the core, the European wine segment faces direct tariff cost. Lower confidence given divestitures.

headlines: Trump threatens EU with ‘much higher’ tariffs if no trade deal signed by new deadline
